title: Render the active Codex source-repo fallback from the shared catalog
action:
Repair only the live source-repository Codex project fallback, per D1/D2 and decisions 5e6582af/d91a8398. Before any edit, run and record `codex plugin list --json`; if it is unavailable, unparseable, or shows any installed/enabled bee plugin, return `[BLOCKED]` with zero edits. Reproduce the exact old Stop failure with `CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` unset and record exit 1 plus MODULE_NOT_FOUND for `/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs` as red_failure_evidence. Capture the pre-cell hashes of hooks/hooks.json and hooks/claude-hooks.json. Parameterize the catalog renderer as `renderProjection(runtime, { target: "plugin" | "repo" })` (and the text renderer likewise), keeping `plugin` as the default so every existing caller and both existing projections remain byte-identical. Add the Codex repo-target rendering only; do not invent a Claude host-repo transport. Each repo command must resolve the git root from Codex's session cwd, validate that Node and the source wrapper are launchable, emit a stable visible diagnostic and exit 0 when pre-wrapper transport setup is unavailable, then execute `hooks/bee-*.mjs --source=repo` without either Claude root variable. Wrapper exit 0 and deliberate deny exit 2 must pass through unchanged. Add a targeted drift row comparing the repo-target rendering byte-for-byte with .codex/hooks.json. Land that row and renderer first while leaving .codex/hooks.json untouched, run it, and checkpoint the expected RED mismatch in the cell trace; only then regenerate .codex/hooks.json from the renderer and make it GREEN. Do not hand-author event groups. Finish by proving `git diff --exit-code -- hooks/hooks.json hooks/claude-hooks.json`, rerunning the plugin census, and running the full verification. Never modify trust state; changed definitions are reviewed by the human at Gate 4.

STRICT-FLAG PREREQUISITE (validated 2026-07-12, do not skip): the contract runner currently IGNORES unrecognized CLI flags and still exits 0 printing ALL PASS — `node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag` exits 0 today. That makes this cell's own verify (`--catalog-only`) and 6b's (`--repo-route-only`) pass GREEN even if the mode is never implemented, which is the exact defect that got the predecessor cell codex-parity-6 rejected. So: make the runner's argument parsing STRICT FIRST — an unknown flag prints the known modes (--baseline, --catalog-only) to stderr and exits non-zero. IMPORTANT: --catalog-only ALREADY EXISTS as a real mode (hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs, documented at line 31, built by cell codex-parity-2; it runs the catalog drift-check rows and today exits 0 ALL PASS). Do NOT rebuild it — EXTEND it with this cell's repo-target drift row. The only NEW argv work is strict rejection of unknown flags. Show the strict-flag row RED before it goes GREEN, and keep the BARE no-flag default run green — it is in this cell's verify precisely because rewriting argv parsing is the fastest way to break the 71-row default path that everything else depends on.

truths: .codex/hooks.json is reproduced byte-for-byte by the shared catalog's Codex repo-target renderer, so hand drift turns the suite red
all nine generated repo commands contain no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT reference and launch the current source wrappers with source identity repo

PINNED FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT (do not paraphrase): when the git root cannot be resolved, the rendered command writes the EXACT literal `bee: hook transport unavailable (no git root)` to STDERR, writes NOTHING to stdout, and exits 0. stderr is mandatory because stdout on a Stop hook must parse as JSON systemMessage (codex-parity-6b truth 3) — a diagnostic on stdout would break that same command. The literal is pinned so 6b can assert it mechanically; a bare `[ -n "$r" ] || exit 0` is SILENT and violates spec R2 (fail-open must be visible, never silent)
wrapper success exit 0 and deliberate deny exit 2 pass through the transport unchanged
the new repo-config drift assertion is recorded RED against the old checked-in .codex/hooks.json before regeneration and GREEN afterward

the contract runner REJECTS an unrecognized CLI flag with a non-zero exit — today it silently ignores unknown flags and still prints ALL PASS (verified: `node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag` exits 0), which means any verify naming a not-yet-implemented mode passes GREEN against a broken tree

REQUIRED-ROW MANIFEST: the runner fails non-zero when a required row id is ABSENT or SKIPPED. Today `failures = results.filter(r => !r.pass)` (test_hook_contracts.mjs:1022) while skip rows are constructed with `pass: true` (:626-628), so a SKIPPED row prints ALL PASS and exits 0 — a worker could register the new repo-target drift row as `skip: true` ("codex CLI unavailable") and cap green having done nothing. Declare row id `codex-repo-target-drift` as REQUIRED in the --catalog-only group: absent or skipped => non-zero exit
the new repo-target drift row is registered inside the --catalog-only group (not only in the default suite), so this cell's own verify actually executes it
hooks/hooks.json and hooks/claude-hooks.json remain byte-identical to their pre-cell forms, enforced by git diff plus recorded hashes
a parseable before/after plugin census shows no installed or enabled bee plugin; unknown or duplicate state blocks with zero edits

artifacts.substantive: target-parameterized renderer with a deterministic Codex source-repo transport and unchanged plugin default
generated active project fallback using git-root resolution and current source wrappers
repo-target byte-drift row that demonstrably fails on the old active config

key_links: .codex/hooks.json is generated only by renderProjectionText(codex, { target: repo })
repo-target commands resolve to hooks/bee-*.mjs, whose shared adapter delegates to the repository bee helpers
plugin target remains the renderer default, preserving every existing caller and checked-in plugin projection
prohibitions: do not install, enable, disable, configure, trust, or bypass any Codex plugin or hook outside the three bounded files
do not edit hooks/hooks.json, hooks/claude-hooks.json, onboarding, installers, plugin manifests, .claude settings, vendored .bee/bin/hooks, or user/global configuration
do not fork wrapper implementations, duplicate event groups by hand, add a features.hooks opt-in requirement, or change plugin-side source identity
do not add native Windows commandWindows or non-POSIX-shell transport in this WSL/bash incident cell
do not touch concurrent .bee/config.json, dispatch-log, spike, E3/E4, or fanout-delegation work

verify: node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --catalog-only && ! node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && git diff --exit-code ba31819cea42e3f4dcfd294f05778caac21d8b5d -- hooks/hooks.json hooks/claude-hooks.json && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Repaired the live Codex source-repo hook fallback: catalog renderer parameterized as renderProjection(runtime, {target: plugin|repo}) with plugin as default (both checked-in projections byte-identical, hashes unchanged); .codex/hooks.json regenerated from renderProjectionText(codex, {target:repo}) — 9 commands that resolve the git root from Codex's session cwd, fail open VISIBLY on stderr with the pinned literal, and exec hooks/bee-*.mjs --source=repo with no Claude root variable. Contract runner hardened first: STRICT argv (unknown flag => exit 2, was exit 0 ALL PASS) and a REQUIRED-ROW MANIFEST (codex-repo-target-drift absent or skipped => non-zero), both proved to bite. Drift row recorded RED against the unmodified .codex/hooks.json, GREEN only after regeneration.

deviations.what: The cell action says the repo command must 'validate that Node and the source wrapper are launchable'. I rendered EXACTLY the transport shape pinned by the Gate-3 validation report and the cell's PINNED FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT truth (git-root guard + pinned stderr literal + exit 0), and added no extra node/wrapper existence probes.
deviations.why: The pinned truth and the orchestrator brief both say render exactly this shape, do not redesign it; codex-parity-6b asserts the literal and the branch set mechanically. git rev-parse failing covers BOTH 'not a repo' and 'git absent from PATH' (both proved: exit 0 + literal + empty stdout), which is the entire pre-wrapper transport setup step. Inventing additional guard branches with unpinned diagnostics would diverge from what 6b asserts.

deviations.what: Added a second new row, catalog-drift :: codex-repo-target-transport, beyond the single required drift row.
deviations.why: The cell's truth 2 ('all nine generated repo commands contain no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT and launch the current source wrappers with source identity repo') and the pinned fail-open truth were otherwise only asserted by the byte-drift row, which would silently accept a wrong-but-consistent rendering. This row asserts the transport contract structurally: 9 commands, no Claude root vars, --source=repo, visible stderr fail-open.

trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs — existing --catalog-only mode (line 31, cell codex-parity-2): 6 rows, exit 0 ALL PASS before this cell. EXTENDED, not rebuilt.",
    "hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:1022 failures = results.filter(r => !r.pass) vs :626-628 skip rows built with pass:true — the skip-as-pass hole named in the cell's required-row truth. Confirmed live.",
    "argv handling: process.argv.includes(...) only — unknown flags silently ignored, fell through to the default suite."
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "STRICT argv (parseArgv + KNOWN_FLAGS): an unrecognized flag now prints the known modes to stderr and exits 2. Previously `--totally-bogus-flag` exited 0 printing ALL PASS.",
    "REQUIRED-ROW MANIFEST (REQUIRED_CATALOG_ROW_IDS + enforceRequiredRows): row id codex-repo-target-drift is REQUIRED — ABSENT or SKIPPED synthesizes a real FAILING row => non-zero exit. Enforced in BOTH --catalog-only and the default suite.",
    "NEW row catalog-drift :: codex-repo-target-drift — renderProjectionText('codex', {target:'repo'}) must reproduce .codex/hooks.json byte-for-byte.",
    "NEW row catalog-drift :: codex-repo-target-transport — all 9 repo commands: no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, launch hooks/bee-*.mjs --source=repo, visible fail-open diagnostic on stderr."
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "(1) OLD STOP FAILURE (the bug under repair). Command from the pre-cell .codex/hooks.json: `node \"$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs`. With CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset (Codex never sets it): exit=1, Error: Cannot find module '/.bee/bin/hooks/bee-state-sync.mjs', code: 'MODULE_NOT_FOUND'.\n\n(2) CELL VERIFY PRE-CHANGE: exit 1 (RED by design). Failed at `! --totally-bogus-flag` — the bogus flag was ignored, ran the default suite, exited 0 ALL PASS, so the `!` inverted it and short-circuited the chain.\n\n(3) RED CHECKPOINT (renderer + rows landed, .codex/hooks.json still UNMODIFIED): --catalog-only => 8 rows, 1 failing, exit 1. FAIL - catalog-drift :: codex-repo-target-drift :: DRIFT: rendering the logical catalog for \"codex\" at target \"repo\" does NOT reproduce .codex/hooks.json byte-for-byte. Only regenerating the file from the renderer turned it green.\n\n(4) STRICT-FLAG RED->GREEN: before, `node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs --totally-bogus-flag` => exit 0 ALL PASS. After: exit 2 with 'unknown flag(s): --totally-bogus-flag / known modes: --baseline, --catalog-only'.\n\n(5) REQUIRED-ROW MANIFEST PROVED TO BITE (both escape hatches, probed on a scratch copy then restored byte-identically): (a) drift row forced to skip:true => 'FAIL - required-row-present:codex-repo-target-drift :: REQUIRED ROW SKIPPED', exit 1. (b) drift row renamed away (absent) => 'REQUIRED ROW ABSENT', exit 1. A skipped or dropped required row can no longer print ALL PASS.",
  "verification_run": [
    "CELL VERIFY POST-CHANGE: exit 0 (GREEN). --catalog-only 8 rows 0 failing; --totally-bogus-flag rejected (exit 2); bare default suite 73 rows across 11 groups, 0 skipped, 0 failing (was 71 — the 2 new repo rows; default path intact); pinned-ref diff clean; test_lib 169 passed/0 failed; test_onboard PASS 0 failures.",
    "LIVE-FIRE of the GENERATED transport (commands read straight out of the new .codex/hooks.json, run through bash -lc with CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR unset): (1) THE FIX — Stop cmd from a nested cwd inside the repo: exit 0, stdout empty, stderr empty (the old form died MODULE_NOT_FOUND on this exact input). (2) FAIL-OPEN non-git cwd (/tmp): exit 0, stdout EMPTY, stderr exactly `bee: hook transport unavailable (no git root)`. (3) FAIL-OPEN git absent from PATH: exit 0, stdout EMPTY, same pinned literal on stderr. (4) DENY PASS-THROUGH — write-guard on a direct .bee/state.json edit: exit 2 with the direct-edit reason on stderr, unchanged by the transport.",
    "PLUGIN CENSUS before AND after (`codex plugin list --json`, parseable both times): installed = compound-engineering@compound-engineering-plugin only; bee plugins installed/enabled = 0. Never touched trust state.",
    "PLUGIN PROJECTIONS UNCHANGED: post-cell sha256 identical to pre-cell — hooks/hooks.json bae59d42975224068f3295301e8f6ac54a95edc53e2891262f9038d62052ec40, hooks/claude-hooks.json ecd3fcc34f9c9a728a732e2bf7d45ebb9d8cb1e26c2c94ee2ab73db9338887ed; git diff against pinned ref ba31819 clean."
  ]
}
